UI_prof_WindowWasherDesc: Spent your days dangling off skyscrapers with a squeegee. Heights don't scare you - they keep you sane.
UI_trait_WWHeightLover_Desc: You feel at home up high. Time spent at altitude keeps boredom at bay; too long at ground level and the walls start closing in.
UI_WW_Hint_Spawn: Stand on the control panel, then press Page Up / Page Down (or right-click the deck) to move the scaffold.
UI_WW_Tip_Electric: Fast, costs no stamina. Available only while powered (city grid, or a generator covering the winch).
UI_WW_Tip_Manual: Always available. Costs Endurance per floor (going up costs more; overweight makes it worse). Auto-cancels if you're too tired.
UI_WW_Tip_NoPower: No power at the winch. Before the blackout use the grid; afterwards run a generator near the outrigger node. Until then, use the manual winch.
UI_WW_Notebook_P1:
Moving the rig — stand on the center plank of the deck. Nothing moves unless I'm standing on it.

- Page Up = up a floor.
- Page Down = down a floor.
- Or right-click the deck -> Move Scaffold.
UI_WW_Notebook_P2:
The winches —

- Electric. Fast, no sweat. Runs off the building's power. Grid's dead? Hook a generator up near the rig and it's back. No power, no electric.

- Manual. The crank. Always works, never quits. But it wears me out — every floor costs, and going up costs more. Run out of steam and the crank just won't turn. Rest first.
UI_WW_Notebook_P3:
Funny thing — up here I feel fine. Clear head, no boredom, could hang all day. It's the ground that gets to me: the longer I'm stuck down low, the more the walls close in.

Stay high. Stay sane.

Way in is through the windows. Don't look down. Don't fall.
